artifacts.summary: Firecrawl was used for official-domain discovery and content extraction. Verified evidence comes from University of Twente official pages/PDFs: the central AI dossier, the official education PDF, the AI and assessment page, the Generative AI learning-teaching page, and the Executive Board AI statement/news page. Programme-level, login-gated Canvas, and external national resources were not used as evidence for university-wide tracker claims.

artifacts.sourceTitle: AI in education, research and administration
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Can students use AI tools for assignments or theses? That depends on the course. Each programme or lecturer may have their own policy. When in doubt, ask your lecturer. Always use AI transparently and responsibly.
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: Student AI use for assignments or theses depends on the course, programme, or lecturer policy.

artifacts.sourceTitle: AI in education, research and administration
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: How should I handle data when using AI tools? Always assume your input may be stored externally. Never include personal data, student details, research data or confidential material in public AI tools.

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Before using any new application or tool, please note that, according to the Use of Personal Applications Policy, it is not permitted to accept license agreements that are binding for the university without prior approval. artifacts.sourceTitle: Use of AI in Education at the University of Twente
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: It is required that students mention the use of AI. Therefore, when any form of AI is used, the use of these tools should be included in the appendix... Students using AI, without the explicit consent of the instructor and acknowledgement of the tool in an appendix, should therefore be considered to have committed academic misconduct.
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: UT requires AI use disclosure in an appendix and treats unconsented, unacknowledged AI use as academic misconduct.

artifacts.sourceTitle: AI and assessment
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: There are AI detection software, but in general, they have their limits and are not very reliable. There are also privacy, confidentiality, and other issues involved if the work of students or others is put in a system to check.

artifacts.title: University of Twente AI Policy Staged Crawl
artifacts.summary: Staged official-source crawl for University of Twente. The run found central guidance in UT's AI dossier, an official education PDF, learning-teaching assessment guidance, a generative AI education support page, and an Executive Board AI statement. Claims are staged only, agent-reviewed, and not marked canonical.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
The linked Canvas AI resource hub was not used because it may require authenticated course access.
The Computer Science academic-integrity page was rejected for broad claims because it is programme-level guidance.
External national resources linked by UT pages, including SURF, were not used as University of Twente evidence.
artifacts.sourceRightsPolicy: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.
